theme.title: Quiet Session
theme.summary: Technology (+0.32%) and Financials (-0.00%) led the market today, while Utilities (-0.29%) and Communication Svcs (-0.29%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged -0.00%, defensives -0.19%, and value/cyclicals -0.13% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.72%, Dow +0.29%, Nasdaq +1.12% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at +0.45%. The VIX rose 1.67% to 15.82, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 0.05% to $4,139.80, crude oil was little changed at $69.33, and bitcoin slipped 1.47% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
